title: mentha carlovicensis
intro: Mentha carlovicensis, also known as the Watermint or Irish mint, is a perennial plant that belongs to the mint family (Lamiaceae) and is native to Ireland and the UK. It has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic qualities. The health benefits of Mentha carlovicensis include relief from digestive issues, sore throats, and headaches, as well as its ability to reduce stress and anxiety. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be added to skincare products and mouthwashes due to its antiseptic and antifungal properties.
